questions.question: Points $A$ and $B$ lie on a circle with centre $O$ and radius $10\text{ cm}$. If angle $AOB = 70°$, calculate the arc length $AB$.
questions.explanation:
Arc length $= \frac{70}{360} \times 2 \times \pi \times 10 \approx 12.217$.

**Common mistake:** The student might calculate the area ($61.1$) instead of the length.

questions.question: Hence, using the radius of $10\text{ cm}$ and angle of $70°$, find the area of the minor sector $AOB$.
questions.explanation:
Sector area $= \frac{70}{360} \times \pi \times 10^{2} \approx 61.086$.

**Common mistake:** The student may use the diameter ($20$) instead of the radius squared ($100$).

questions.question: A minor arc $MN$. $18\text{ cm}$ is part of a circle with radius $10\text{ cm}$. Calculate the angle $MON$ correct to 1 decimal place.
questions.explanation:
$18 = \frac{\theta}{360} \times 2\pi(10) \Rightarrow 18 = \frac{\theta\pi}{18} \Rightarrow \theta = 324/\pi \approx 103.13°$.

**Common mistake:** The student might forget to include the factor of 2 for the circumference formula.

questions.question: The points $X$ and $Y$ lie on a circle with centre $O$ and radius $8\text{ cm}$. Given angle $XOY = 80°$, calculate the length of the chord $XY$.
questions.explanation:
Use the sine rule in triangle $XOY$: $XY = 2 \times 8 \times \sin(40°) \approx 10.28$.

**Common mistake:** A student might assume the triangle is equilateral and pick $8\text{ cm}$.

questions.question: Find the area of the minor segment of the circle cut off by chord $XY$. So $r=8$ and $\theta=80°$).
questions.explanation:
Sector area $= \frac{80}{360} \times \pi \times 8^2 \approx 44.68$. Triangle area $= 0.5 \times 8 \times 8 \times \sin 80° \approx 31.51$. Segment area $= 44.68 - 31.51 = 13.17$.

**Common mistake:** The student might calculate the triangle area ($31.5$) or sector area ($44.7$) and stop there.
